name: Curried Pumpkin and Ginger Scones
recipeIngredient: 3 Cups King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour
1 Stick cold butter, cut into eight pieces
1/2 Cup cooked, puréed pumpkin or squash
1 Cup buttermilk, plain yogurt or sour cream (low-fat or nonfat is fine)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, ginger, curry powder, and turmeric. Add sugar.
2. Cut in the butter with a pastry blender, your fingers, or an electric mixer until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the pumpkin or squash and buttermilk/yogurt/sour cream until smooth. Stir into the dry ingredients until just combined.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured work surface. Pat or roll it into a 10-inch square that is about 1/2 to 3/4 inches thick. Cut into 2-inch squares.
5. Transfer scones to a parchment-lined or lightly greased baking sheet, leaving about an inch between each one.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 425°F for 20 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from the oven and serve warm or at room temperature. Yield: 25 scones.
